item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this well-enough. Not too long; not longer than it needs to be. Not edgier than it needs to be. Quality work all around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the play on the honey panacea scam and the funk. Monteparnas seems to be saying the gravestone/coffin transportation is a Chekhov's gun situation, but I disagree. The ending of the story clearly reveals one mysterious way in which the bodies are sourced. I also like that this is not 100% doom and gloom, since the effects are seemingly positive and these entities may be content immortals enjoying a benign afterlife/second life. There's still that menace/sickening factor in the cannibalistic element though, which gives it an appropriate SCP flavor.

One tweak I'd humbly recommend since Dr Calder described a similar issue: when the log starts, I was a bit thrown off by the names. It may just be me, but it seemed at first like the agents were listening in on a conversation in the house due to the casual tone and use of pronouns. Shortly after, I realized the two speakers were the agents, but it briefly took me out of the article. Maybe put Agent in front of their names or something like that? It would also help fix the issue Dr Calder had with similar names.

I'll +1 anyway, just some thoughts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's good, I enjoyed it but, bits of it felt like they were just there for seemingly no reason and its log was a bit confusing, one of the agents and the con man had similarly spelled names, and it may just be a dyslexic thing but I had to read the log twice to realize that there were to people with similar names that would be the only thing needs changing to make it clearer to read without confusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I clicked on "Underread & Underrated", saw the Lemon Demon reference, jumped in my seat, quickly skimmed through the article and immediately slammed a +1. Now to actually read this in-depth :')

but in all seriousness, Lemon Demon songs are like perfect SCP material 👀

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed reading that. It is such an original idea (Not that I'm saying that other SCP aren't) but this particular one caught my eye, the karaoke thing, and the honey persons just sound really interesting, like, it really catches your eye. I personally didn't like the log that much, seems a little bit goofy, but overall it feels great.
You have my upvote +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is a good article, but there are some glaring problems.

The Containment makes it clear and all-too-relevant that gravestones and coffins are transplanted from somewhere else to some other place, but the description gives nothing about it. Although the log does include a coffin it doesn't disappear either, so there is no relation whatsoever between the article and its containment procedures (apart from keeping people from entering).

Also it is said that the con artist evaded the Foundation in the past, but stops on that and it doesn't have any impact in the story whatsoever, so it must either be elaborated on or removed, IMHO, 'cause it does stand out.

Some info on the prices at the bar itself would be good, although it already made it clear that it costs actual common money. I would argue for a more thorough investigation on all the things, but it doesn't really need it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love how you incorporated elements of the song into this article. You even added the funky tunes! +1

item.title: SWEET POST
item.content:encoded:
I'm digging up your coffin and pouring out the contents
Your sexy sweet solution is prime for distribution
SWEET BOD...

Sweet Bod · Lemon Demon

This was sweet to write. Hopefully it's sweet to read.

For those of you unfamiliar with mellified man, click here.

Critique Peeps:

  • Deadly Bread — SCP-5522
  • DrAkimoto — SCP-5508
  • Dr Moned — It started with a bang. Or did it?
  • Popsioak — SCP-5425
  • Westrin — SCP-5970

Image Source:

  • sweet.jpg — CC BY-SA 2.0, I don't know how I feel about using an image of real people's graves in a jokey scp like this so may remove it.

« ANGRY PEOPLE || SPIRIT PHONE SHUFFLE: SWEET BOD || NO EYED GIRL »
